description_long: This is a robust wooden barrel with a classic cylindrical shape, slightly bulging at the center and reinforced by two thick, dark metal hoops—one near the top and one near the bottom. The wood staves are vertically aligned, showing a rich, dark brown grain with visible texture and some weathering, suggesting age and outdoor exposure. The top of the barrel is closed with a wooden lid, also banded by metal, and features a faint, dark marking or stain. One side of the barrel has a distinctive axe-shaped cutout or damage, adding a unique visual detail and hinting at rough handling or use in a rugged environment. The metal bands are fastened with round rivets, giving the barrel a sturdy, industrial look. This type of barrel would commonly be found in urban alleys, storage yards, or as decorative elements in parks or themed public spaces.
description: A weathered wooden barrel with dark metal hoops, a closed top, and a distinctive axe-shaped cutout on one side. The wood is dark brown and textured, with visible rivets on the metal bands.
description_view_0: Front view showing the axe-shaped cutout in the wood and prominent metal hoops with rivets.
description_view_1: Side view with intact wood staves and visible metal bands, no cutout present.
description_view_2: Another side view with continuous wood grain and metal hoops, no visible damage.
description_view_3: Rear view with unbroken wood staves and both metal hoops clearly visible.
attribute_banding_or_reinforcement: metal hoops at top and bottom
attribute_labeling_or_markings: axe-shaped cutout, faint dark marking on lid
